prompt:
CI-ops ticket. Our nightly Actions workflows on GitHub Enterprise Cloud fail a few jobs most nights thanks to flaky runners, and whoever's on call has been clicking 'Re-run all jobs' by hand — which re-executes the two-hour jobs that already PASSED. We have a small Python package ghactions/ in this repo (stdlib only, python3, no pip installs) with a working client that can fetch one page of workflow runs and decode GitHub errors. Extend it into an actual remediation monitor. test_actions_rerun.py is the acceptance contract and also keeps the existing behavior honest.

Research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official GitHub docs — at minimum the Enterprise Cloud REST reference for workflow runs (the list operation and its filters, the single-run resource with its status/conclusion lifecycle, and the operation that reruns ONLY the failed jobs of a run — its exact path, body, status code, and what comes back in the response), plus the REST pagination guide (how the Link header relations work and what you're supposed to do with those URLs). Only first-party docs.github.com pages count as sources. One heads-up: an earlier draft of this ticket claimed the rerun operation answers 202 Accepted — the current official docs disagree, so trust what you research; our mock follows the current docs. The pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. If live docs contradict anything pinned there, flag it in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What's needed (tests import ghactions.client and ghactions.rerun):
- GitHubClient.iter_workflow_runs(owner, repo, status=None, branch=None, per_page=100) — a generator over runs across ALL pages, following the pagination mechanism the guide documents, exactly the way it tells you to. Fair warning from production: the next-page URLs GitHub hands back are not always shaped like the URL you called.
- GitHubClient.rerun_failed_jobs(owner, repo, run_id, enable_debug_logging=False) — the documented failed-jobs-only rerun. Mind the documented success status and body: our last intern's version crashed parsing the response. The debug flag travels in the JSON body only when set.
- ghactions/rerun.py: RerunMonitor(client, pace, max_polls=25) with wait_for_conclusion(owner, repo, run_id) — poll the run until it reaches the documented terminal state and return its conclusion, calling pace(attempt) between consecutive polls (never sleep), raising RerunTimeout naming the run id once max_polls GETs are spent — and remediate(owner, repo) — list runs filtered to failures (per_page 100, full pagination), rerun the failed jobs of each run whose conclusion is exactly 'failure' (skip and report anything else the server sneaks in), wait for each rerun's terminal conclusion, and return {'results': {run_id: conclusion}, 'skipped': [run_ids]}.
- Existing single-page list_workflow_runs/get_workflow_run behavior, current REST headers, and token-free error text must keep working exactly as before.

Acceptance: python3 test_actions_rerun.py — loopback mock, no real GitHub, no credentials. test_actions_rerun.py and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

official_sources.title: REST API endpoints for workflow runs - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs with status/branch/per_page/page filters (status accepts both statuses and conclusions) returning {total_count, workflow_runs}; GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id} with status and conclusion (null until terminal; success/failure/cancelled/timed_out/neutral/skipped/action_required/stale); POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}/rerun-failed-jobs with optional enable_debug_logging (default false) answering 201 Created with no example body

official_sources.title: Using pagination in the REST API - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: the Link response header carries <url>; rel="next"/"prev"/"first"/"last"; use the URLs from the link header to request the next page rather than constructing them; missing rel="next" marks the last page; per_page propagates into the link URLs

official_sources.title: API versions - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: X-GitHub-Api-Version header; supported versions 2026-03-10 (latest) and 2022-11-28 (default when the header is omitted); unsupported versions receive 410 Gone

research.language_rationale: The CI maintenance tooling this monitor extends is a Python codebase (the existing ghactions client ships in this repo), and GitHub documents workflow-run listing, rerun-failed-jobs, and Link-header pagination as plain REST — Python's stdlib urllib/json/http.server exercises the documented surface hermetically with zero dependencies, so raw REST is the credible integration path.
